mechanics.name: Job select
mechanics.summary: Equipment that enters by creating a 1/1 colorless Hero token and attaching to it; the equipped creature gains a class and abilities. With 27 Equipment and ~23 Hero payoffs, Equipment density is a real draft axis — a Job select piece is never a dead draw.

mechanics.name: Summons
mechanics.summary: 'Summon:' cards are Enchantment Creature — Saga: a body that ticks a chapter of value each turn and then leaves. Several spells and DFCs cheat them in transformed; they reward decks that can protect a short-lived attacker.

mechanics.name: Towns
mechanics.summary: A Land — Town subtype (23 lands); several payoffs count Towns you control or unlock when enough are assembled, making otherwise-plain nonbasics draftable role-players.

mechanics.name: Transform legends
mechanics.summary: 27 transform cards — legendary characters flipping into upgraded forms (Cecil, Garland // Chaos, Exdeath // Neo Exdeath). The flip conditions shape gameplay: most ask you to attack, cast, or hit a threshold.

mechanics.name: Graveyard spells
mechanics.summary: Flashback (14), Surveil (11), and Mill (10) form a spells-from-the-graveyard axis in blue/black/red — stock the yard, then recast for value.

mechanics.name: Tiered
mechanics.summary: Modal spells with escalating additional costs (Fire/Fira/Firaga): scale one card from early interaction to a late-game haymaker; Treasure and ramp make the top tier real.

mechanics.name: Lands matter
mechanics.summary: Landfall (10) plus Landcycling/Typecycling (10) form the green lands axis; cycling lands away also fuels the graveyard themes.

archetypes.name: Magitek artifacts
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with cheap artifacts — Job select Equipment, Vehicles, and Magitek bodies — then cash them in with count-based payoffs like Machinist's Arsenal and Adelbert Steiner. Curve out and attack, using taps, bounce, and White Auracite for tempo; artifact count, not card quality, is the engine.
keyCards: Dion, Bahamut's Dominant // Bahamut, Warden of Light
Jill, Shiva's Dominant // Shiva, Warden of Ice

archetypes.name: Sacrifice aristocrats
archetypes.playstyle: Feed cheap Heroes, tokens, and spare artifacts to sacrifice outlets and stack the death triggers into drains, counters, and card draw; a removal-dense midrange deck that wins attrition wars rather than races.
payoffs: Sephiroth, Fabled SOLDIER // Sephiroth, One-Winged Angel
keyCards: Dion, Bahamut's Dominant // Bahamut, Warden of Light
Sidequest: Hunt the Mark // Yiazmat, Ultimate Mark

archetypes.name: Job select Equipment aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Curve out cheap bodies and Job select Equipment — each weapon brings its own 1/1 Hero to wield it — then pressure with payoffs that scale on Equipment count like Adelbert Steiner and Slash of Light. Keep attacking; equip triggers and combat tricks out-tempo any defense.
keyCards: Dion, Bahamut's Dominant // Bahamut, Warden of Light
Joshua, Phoenix's Dominant // Phoenix, Warden of Fire

archetypes.name: Summons go-wide
archetypes.playstyle: Chain cheap Summon Sagas and token makers into a wide lifelinking board, with Garnet cashing lore counters and Yuna making your enchantment creatures permanent trampling threats. It plays as a value midrange deck that grinds Saga chapters, not a pure token rush.
keyCards: Dion, Bahamut's Dominant // Bahamut, Warden of Light

archetypes.name: Graveyard value control
archetypes.playstyle: Grind the long game with removal and 2-for-1s while surveil, flashback, and loots stock your graveyard, turning Swallowed by Leviathan into a hard counter and flipping Card Collection, Ultimecia, and Emet-Selch. The theme is soft — removal density and bombs win, graveyard payoffs are the topping.
payoffs: Emet-Selch, Unsundered // Hades, Sorcerer of Eld
keyCards: Jill, Shiva's Dominant // Shiva, Warden of Ice
Sephiroth, Fabled SOLDIER // Sephiroth, One-Winged Angel
Zenos yae Galvus // Shinryu, Transcendent Rival
Sidequest: Hunt the Mark // Yiazmat, Ultimate Mark

archetypes.name: Big-spell wizards
archetypes.playstyle: Land cheap payoff creatures, then chain four-plus-mana noncreature spells — Tiered magic and X spells count total mana spent — to snowball counters, stuns, Treasures, and card draw. Grinds to a mid-game where every big spell is removal plus a threat boost.
signposts: The Emperor of Palamecia // The Lord Master of Hell
keyCards: Jill, Shiva's Dominant // Shiva, Warden of Ice

archetypes.name: Town ramp
archetypes.playstyle: Ramp with land-fetch commons and Towns into a fat top end, converting extra land drops into landfall counters and Town-count payoffs like Travel the Overworld and Balamb Garden. Bounce and counters buy time until the haymakers take over.
payoffs: Balamb Garden, SeeD Academy // Balamb Garden, Airborne
keyCards: Jill, Shiva's Dominant // Shiva, Warden of Ice

archetypes.name: Black Mage spellslinger
archetypes.playstyle: Amass 0/1 Black Mage Wizard tokens, then chain cheap noncreature spells so every removal spell doubles as reach; win long grindy games on premium black interaction while the pingers finish the job.
signposts: Garland, Knight of Cornelia // Chaos, the Endless
payoffs: Kuja, Genome Sorcerer // Trance Kuja, Fate Defied
keyCards: Sephiroth, Fabled SOLDIER // Sephiroth, One-Winged Angel
Sidequest: Hunt the Mark // Yiazmat, Ultimate Mark

archetypes.name: Self-mill graveyard
archetypes.playstyle: Stock your graveyard with permanents via mill-on-a-body commons, then cash the count in for Cloud of Darkness blowouts, Neo Exdeath flips, and Evil Reawakened rebuys. Premium black removal and chunky green bodies let it grind as a plain midrange deck when the engine doesn't come together.
signposts: Exdeath, Void Warlock // Neo Exdeath, Dimension's End
keyCards: Sephiroth, Fabled SOLDIER // Sephiroth, One-Winged Angel
Sidequest: Hunt the Mark // Yiazmat, Ultimate Mark

archetypes.name: Chocobo landfall
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with 2/2 Chocobo tokens and landfall creatures, then let every land drop pump the team for wide, trampling attacks. Land-fetch spells and Summons keep triggers flowing and ramp you into oversized top-end.

combos.note: Gilgamesh dumps Equipment straight onto the board and Weapons Vendor suits up a threat with them for free each combat
The Arsenal is a free +1/+1 on Adelbert that also brings its own Hero to hold it
Weapons Vendor moves the Arsenal onto your biggest body each combat for another artifact-count pump
Sacrifice Zack in response to removal to shift the Arsenal and his counters onto an indestructible attacker
Every creature or artifact death both grows Gabranth and drains the opponent through Al Bhed Salvagers
Four Hero tokens become sacrifice fuel that pile +1/+1 counters onto Gabranth
Sacrificing Zack to save a creature also triggers Al Bhed Salvagers' drain
Garnet cashes the lore counters off your Summon Sagas into permanent counters on herself
Yuna recurs a spent Summon Saga and makes your enchantment-creature Summons trampling lifelinkers
Loot a bomb into the yard with Locke, then Evil Reawakened brings it back with two extra counters
Locke's combat looting stocks the creature-filled graveyard that The Final Days' flashback empties into a token army
Cast Fire Magic at the Firaga tier and Shantotto swings for all that mana plus draws a card
Vivi ramps into and pings off every Fire Magic, snowballing counters and reach

cards: The Emperor of Palamecia // The Lord Master of Hell
combos.note: Each four-plus-mana Fira/Firaga stacks a counter toward flipping the Emperor into a demon
A Firaga-tier cast clears the four-mana bar for a free Prompto Treasure
Circle of Power adds a Wizard pinger so each noncreature spell burns twice over alongside Black Waltz
A board of Wizard tokens turns every noncreature spell into a barrage of pings
Town Greeter's mill fills the graveyard so Cloud of Darkness wipes a big creature on entry

cards: Exdeath, Void Warlock // Neo Exdeath, Dimension's End
combos.note: Summon: Titan's mill-five races Exdeath to its six-permanent transform
Each land drop both spawns a Racetrack Bird and hands a creature +2/+2 and trample off Gladiolus
Traveling Chocobo doubles Racetrack's landfall so every land makes two Birds
Town Greeter digs up the Towns that make Travel the Overworld a cheap draw-four

cards: Balamb Garden, SeeD Academy // Balamb Garden, Airborne
combos.note: A pile of Towns both flips Balamb Garden cheaply and slashes Travel the Overworld's cost
Cloud doubles the triggered abilities of Equipment attached to it, so Thief's Knife's combat-damage draw fires twice per hit — and Cloud even tutors the Knife up on entry.
The two l'Cie meld into Ragnarok, Divine Deliverance once both are on the battlefield together.
Cloud makes equips targeting it cost {2} less, so Samurai's Katana attaches for {3} and turns the otherwise-plain Cloud into a hasty, trampling, double-striking, indestructible threat.
Firion token-copies Dragoon's Lance as it enters and the copy's own Job select spawns a second Hero and equips it — two hasty flying Knights off one card.
Rydia's Summon ability reanimates Summon: Titan from the graveyard with haste to re-run its chapters as an attacker.
Ultimecia's repeated Surveil 2 draws a card each time through Matoya while stocking the graveyard toward Ultimecia's eight-card transform.
Casting Thunder Magic's six-mana Thundaga tier pumps Shantotto +6/+0 and, as a 4+-mana noncreature spell, also draws a card.

cards: The Emperor of Palamecia // The Lord Master of Hell
combos.note: The Emperor taps for spell-only mana to fund Ice Magic's six-mana Blizzaga, which as a 4+-mana noncreature spell also puts it a counter closer to transforming.

cards: Sephiroth, Fabled SOLDIER // Sephiroth, One-Winged Angel
combos.note: Sephiroth's repeatable sacrifice-a-creature deaths each drop a +1/+1 counter on Gabranth while draining the opponent.
Beatrix's combat-step attach moves Ninja's Blades off its 1/1 Hero onto a real body that keeps the loot-and-drain trigger, while the Hero stays on the board.

cards: Kuja, Genome Sorcerer // Trance Kuja, Fate Defied
combos.note: Mysidian Elder's Wizard token helps reach Kuja's four-Wizard transform, after which Trance Kuja doubles every Wizard token's spell-triggered ping.
Y'shtola's end-step exile-and-return blinks Ignis, re-triggering its land ramp and six-card dig every turn.
Reach the Horizon drops two lands at once, firing Gladiolus's landfall twice for +2/+2 and trample each time.
